title: Tripartite Interactions Induced Strongly Correlated Quantum Emissions
authors: Qian Bin, Ying Wu, Franco Nori, Xin-You Lü
abstract: Efficient generation of multiquanta emission is crucial for quantum information processing but remains challenging due to its typical reliance on higher-order quantum processes. Here, we theoretically demonstrate strongly correlated photon-phonon emission enabled by direct tripartite interaction. This interaction facilitates the formation of high-order multiquanta states without more intermediate state transitions, thereby avoiding the suppressed transition rates associated with multiple sequential processes and substantially improving resonant transitions. As a result, high-efficiency strongly correlated even-quanta emission (e.g., two photons and two phonons) can be achieved in the presences of dissipation. Beyond that, we show that introducing two-photon dissipation enables strongly correlated odd-quanta emission (e.g., two photons and one phonon) in the tripartite interaction system by parity-protected suppression of single-photon loss and reconstruction of higher-order multiquanta processes. Our work extends multiquanta emission into the tripartite coupling regime and holds promising potential for applications in hybrid quantum networks.
summary: This paper theoretically demonstrates that direct tripartite interactions in a cavity-atom-mechanical system can efficiently generate strongly correlated multiquanta photon-phonon emissions. By analyzing the system's parity structure and engineering two-photon dissipation, the authors show a pathway to control emission, enabling both even- and odd-quanta correlated states. This has significant implications for building advanced hybrid quantum network components.
main_problem: The paper addresses the challenge of efficiently generating strongly correlated multiquanta quantum emissions, which typically requires complex, higher-order quantum processes.
main_result: Direct tripartite interactions enable high-efficiency multiquanta emission by bypassing intermediate state transitions, and engineered two-photon dissipation allows for the controlled generation of odd-quanta states.
method: The study uses theoretical modeling, deriving effective Hamiltonians and transition rates using perturbation theory (Fermi's Golden Rule) and analyzing system dynamics via master equations.
model_or_system: The system is a hybrid quantum setup comprising a cavity mode, a two-level atom, and a mechanical oscillator, coupled via a direct tripartite interaction. The dynamics are governed by a Hamiltonian and analyzed using master equations.
key_observables: Strongly correlated even-quanta and odd-quanta emission rates, photon-phonon cross-correlations ($g^{(2)}_{ab}$), and effective multiquanta transition rates ($W_{	ext{eff}}$).
important_parameters: Coupling strengths ($\lambda$), detunings ($\Delta_a, \omega_b$), driving amplitudes ($\Omega$), and dissipation rates ($\kappa$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the rotating-wave approximation (RWA) and the assumption that the two-boson-mode subsystem preserves parity symmetry.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate system schematics, energy spectra showing avoided crossings, time evolution of state populations, and comparisons between analytically derived and numerically simulated effective transition rates.
paper_structure: The paper builds from establishing the tripartite interaction model, demonstrating even-quanta emission under dissipation, and then crucially extending the analysis to show how engineered two-photon dissipation enables the generation of odd-quanta emission.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to AMO physics and quantum optics as it provides a concrete theoretical framework for enhancing quantum light sources by engineering interactions (tripartite coupling) and dissipation channels (two-photon loss).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic of the system: a coherently driven two- level atom is trapped in a harmonic potential V (x) at a node of the cavity field. The cavity photon mode (a), the atom (σ), and the mechanical center-of-mass phonon mode (b) are simultaneously coupled. (b,c) State transitions within the (c) even-parity and (d) odd-parity subspaces of the two-boson- mode subsystem, where N = 0, 1, 2 . . . denotes the bosonic exaction number.

FIG. 2. Energy spectrum of the Hamiltonian H as a func- tion of Ω/ωb. (b,c) Enlarged views of the red-boxed re- gions in (a). (d,e) Time evolution of the state populations Pnml = |⟨nml|ψ(t)⟩|2 (l = ± and n, m = 0, 1, 2) at multi- quanta resonances in the absence of dissipation. System pa- rameters: ∆a/ωb = 1.6, λ/ωb = 0.15, (d) Ω/ωb = 1.3, and (e) Ω/ωb = 2.6.

FIG. 3. (a,b) Photon and phonon emission spectra Sa,b when λ/ωb = 0.15. Insets: equal-time second-order cross correlations g(2) ab versus λ/ωb. (c,d) Effective multiquanta transition rates W eff 11,22/ω2 b versus λ/ωb, obtained from full- numerical simulations (black circles) and analytical solutions (red curves). (e,f) Average number of multiquanta emission events ¯ NT versus W eff 11,22/ω2 b (from full-numerical simulations) over a time window T. The left and right columns corre- sponds to multiquanta resonances at Ω/ωb ≈1.3 and Ω/ωb ≈ 2.6, respectively. Other system parameters: ∆a/ωb = 1.6, (a,e)κa = κb = 10γ = 0.25ωb, (b,f)κa = κb = 10γ = 0.025ωb, (e) ωbT = 105, and (f) ωbT = 106.

FIG. 4. A tiny fraction of quantum trajectory in the multi- quanta emission regimes of single photons and single phonons |11−⟩→|00+⟩represented by population dynamics of differ- ent states for (a) single-photon dissipation case and (b) two- photon dissipation case. A (two) yellow (blue) arrow corre- sponding to a (two) single photon (phonon) emission, and the black arrow represents the atom decay. System parameters: ∆a/ωb = 1.6, λ/ωb = 0.15, κa = κa2 = κb = 10γ = 0.25ωb, and Ω/ωb = 1.3.
